**Gasgoo Munich-** What moved the needle in the new-energy vehicle market last week?

**CATL Tech Day: A flood of new products from Shenxing, Qilin, Freevoy, and Naxtra lines**

On April 21, CATL hosted its "Super Tech Day" launch in Beijing, unveiling a raft of new products at once: the third-generation Shenxing ultra-fast charging battery, the third-generation Qilin battery, the Qilin condensed-state battery, the second-generation Freevoy super range-extender/hybrid battery, a new Naxtra battery, and a plan for an integrated super-swapping charging network covering all scenarios.

![宁德科技日.jpg](https://imagecn.gasgoo.com/moblogo/News/UEditor/image/20260425/6391275500651167351250538.jpg)

CATL's third-generation Shenxing ultra-fast charging battery pushes charging speeds to new heights. At room temperature, charging from 10% to 98% state of charge (SOC) takes just 6 minutes and 27 seconds—equivalent to 10C and peaking at 15C, a new global record. Low-temperature performance is equally striking: at -30°C, a 20% to 98% charge takes about 9 minutes. By reducing heat generation, enhancing heat dissipation, and improving precision, CATL ensures the battery retains over 90% of its capacity after 1,000 full cycles.

The third-generation Qilin battery focuses on being "lighter, stronger, and more premium," boasting an energy density of 280Wh/kg. It offers 1,000 kilometers of range with standard 10C fast charging, yet the battery pack weighs only 625kg. Compared to lithium iron phosphate (LFP) vehicles in the same class with 1,000-kilometer range, it sheds 255kg and saves 112 liters of space.

The Qilin condensed-state battery brings aviation-grade condensed-state technology to passenger vehicles for the first time. With a cell energy density of 350Wh/kg—a record for mass-produced batteries—it has already completed maiden flight verification on a 4-ton commercial aircraft. Volumetric energy density reaches 760Wh/L, enabling a range of 1,500 kilometers for sedans and breaking the 1,000-kilometer barrier for full-size SUVs. On safety, the condensed-state electrolyte eliminates risks at the source, achieving "no liquid to leak and no liquid to burn."

The second-generation Freevoy super range-extender/hybrid battery extends pure electric range to 600 kilometers. The LFP version offers 500km, while the NCM version hits 600km; both come standard with 10C fast charging. Its proprietary "super hybrid system" blends ternary and LFP materials at the particle level, achieving an energy density of 230Wh/kg.

The new Naxtra battery signals the shift from technological breakthrough to mass production. This year, CATL overcame four major challenges: extreme water control, hard carbon gas generation, aluminum foil bonding, and the scaling of self-generated anodes. Mass production is slated for the end of 2026.

**Gasgoo Commentary:** On the power battery track, CATL's technology remains formidable.

**Changan to integrate DEEPAL and AVATR by year-end**

On April 21, at a briefing following Changan Automobile Group's global strategy launch, Zhu Huarong, Party Secretary and Chairman of Changan Automobile, announced a strategic integration of its two new-energy brands, AVATR and DEEPAL. The full integration is expected to be completed by the end of this year.

According to Zhu, AVATR and DEEPAL will remain independent at the front end but synergize in the mid-to-back end, preserving independent brand operations. The aim is to build a mid-to-high-end brand group surpassing 1.5 million units globally, with overseas sales accounting for over 40%. Furthermore, the integration will create a "mid-to-high-end brand cluster," boosting efficiency to achieve cost reduction and synergy.

Zhu's high hopes for AVATR and DEEPAL stem from the urgency of future competition in the auto industry. He stated that an automaker needs annual sales of 8 million to 10 million units to truly thrive; 5 million to 7 million units to get by; and 3 million to 3.5 million units is merely the survival line.

"By 2030, Changan Automobile's global sales must guarantee 4 million units and strive for 5 million, with new-energy vehicles accounting for over 60%. Specifically, AVATR and DEEPAL have annual sales targets of 500,000 and 1 million units respectively, while the Changan brand targets 2.6 million," Zhu emphasized.

**Gasgoo Commentary:** The strategic integration of AVATR and DEEPAL marks a critical upgrade in Changan Automobile's layout on the new-energy track, officially launching a strategic transformation focused on efficiency, resource aggregation, and synergistic breakthroughs.

**Geely's 2030 Overseas Strategy: Launching over 10 new-energy vehicle models**

On April 21, at the 2026 International Business Partner Conference, Geely Automobile outlined its overseas market strategy, setting core goals for 2030: launch over 10 new-energy vehicle models abroad, ensure overseas sales account for more than one-third of total volume, achieve a 5% market share, and establish a brand perception of technological leadership in overseas markets.

![图片来源：杨学良.jpg](https://imagecn.gasgoo.com/moblogo/News/UEditor/image/20260425/6391275502806106252832914.jpg)

Image source: Yang Xueliang

The 2030 overseas strategy is built on a clear market foundation and development background. Geely's overall sales target for 2026 is 3.45 million units, with an export target of 640,000 units—a 52% increase from the 420,000 overseas units projected for 2025. Internally, the latest target for 2026 overseas sales has been set at 750,000 units, exceeding 1 million the following year.

To support this overseas strategy, Geely has previously identified three key focus areas. First, treating Southeast Asia as a strategic hub, using the Malaysia AHTV Industrial Park as a bridgehead to build an integrated "R&D-production-sales-service" park with an annual capacity of 500,000 units. This aims to fully radiate into the ASEAN market and solidify the regional foundation.

Second, focusing on developed markets like Europe and the U.S., Geely will adhere to a premium breakthrough strategy. Leveraging Volvo's mature channel advantages and Renault's layout in South America, the company aims to drive scaled development in developed markets.

Third, practicing a diversified and open innovation model for going global. By blending paths such as product export, brand globalization, resource integration, and technology empowerment, Geely will build localized operational systems in key overseas markets, helping the company leap from "Made in China" to a global brand.

Geely has announced an overall goal of exceeding 6.5 million global sales by 2030, with new-energy vehicles accounting for about 75%. Currently, Geely's overseas business has entered a period of rapid development, with overseas sales exceeding 203,000 units in the first quarter of 2026, a 126% year-on-year increase.

**Gasgoo Commentary:** Geely, already thriving at home, is now set to blossom overseas.

**Li L9 Livis debuts at Auto China 2026**

On April 24, Li Auto's embodied AI flagship SUV—the all-new Li L9 Livis—made its global debut at the Auto China 2026. It is scheduled for official market launch and delivery start on May 15.

![微信图片_2026-04-25_224048_125.jpg](https://imagecn.gasgoo.com/moblogo/News/UEditor/image/20260425/6391275504670976777605450.jpg)

Image Li Auto

The Li Auto L9 Livis measures 5,255mm in length, 2,000mm in width, and 1,810mm in height, with a wheelbase of 3,125mm. It maintains the "golden dimensions" of a full-size flagship while preserving urban maneuverability. By shortening the front overhang and extending the rear, combined with a taller hood, a larger bumper, and a wide-shouldered trapezoidal tail design, plus 22-inch wheels, the L9 Livis projects a more commanding presence.

In exterior details, the Li L9 Livis features unique crystal gray chrome trim—costing five times that of traditional trim—to deliver a luxury texture. A unique "diamond ridge" runs from the A-pillar to the D-pillar, offering high recognizability. The L9 Livis adheres to a subtractive design philosophy, replacing ultrasonic radar with more advanced UWB radar and eliminating 12 sensor openings across the body for a flawless surface design.

Simultaneously, the Li L9 Livis is equipped with an 800V active suspension developed specifically for full-size SUVs, and a full steer-by-wire chassis encompassing steer-by-wire steering, rear-wheel steering, and steer-by-wire mechanical braking. This setup aims to break the industry convention that full-size SUVs cannot balance both comfort and handling.

The new model will also feature a 5C range-extender system, the world's first "complete" steer-by-wire chassis, two Mach 100 chips with a total computing power of 2,560 TOPS, and a new-generation interior and smart cockpit. Specific product capabilities will be officially revealed at the launch event on May 15.

**Gasgoo Commentary:** After four years of renewal, the full product strength of the all-new Li Auto L9 Livis is worth anticipating.

**Chery Automobile and CATL build a new "super-swapping integrated" energy ecosystem**

On April 25, Chery Automobile Co., Ltd. (hereinafter "Chery Automobile"), Contemporary Amperex Technology Co., Limited (hereinafter "CATL"), Contemporary Amperex Energy Service Technology Ltd. (hereinafter "CAES"), and Chery Green Energy Ecological Technology Co., Ltd. (hereinafter "Chery Green Energy") signed a strategic cooperation agreement in Beijing. The parties will comprehensively deepen strategic energy cooperation, focusing on areas such as the layout of a super-swapping shared network and the co-construction of a green energy ecosystem, jointly driving the high-quality development of the new-energy industry.

![微信图片_20260425201331_261_1089.jpg](https://imagecn.gasgoo.com/moblogo/News/UEditor/1640-X/20260425/6391275507010038658060168.jpg)

Image source: CATL

According to the agreement, the parties will integrate their technological, industrial, and ecological resources to collaboratively build a digitalized zero-carbon charging and swapping network, providing global users with a convenient experience that supports both charging and swapping. Chery Automobile will develop models compatible with swappable batteries and swapping networks; CATL and its subsidiary CATL Service will be responsible for the development, iteration, and supply assurance of swappable batteries; and Chery Green Energy will provide full-scenario green energy solutions to comprehensively enhance the user experience.

This collaboration will also push "vehicle-energy integration" toward large-scale implementation, promoting green and low-carbon development in the transportation sector through green technological innovation and ecological co-construction, helping to achieve "dual carbon" goals.

**Gasgoo Commentary:** Chery and CATL are joining forces to layout a "battery swapping + energy ecosystem," connecting the closed loop of vehicle, electricity, and grid, opening a new path of charge-and-swap compatibility and green synergy for the new-energy replenishment system.

# Zoomex Announces Today a Major Market Shift as $20.6 Trillion in Liquidity Moves Across Crypto Derivatives Platforms in 2026

[PRESS RELEASE GlobeNewswire](https://markets.businessinsider.com/author/globenewswire)

Apr. 27, 2026, 03:55 AM

![Zoomex](https://ml.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/554e19df-21cf-45e5-911c-3c228968f69e/ahdhgdhs.png)

VICTORIA, Seychelles, April 27, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- [Zoomex](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=7cS9GH4pFlIyTE8tJ60GPVYeQY_OlC4ihaXBbl6Oyu8hjYFuiiVMp3kp9D0Yu97EHdxbNaYNzvKMBfMg1U44og==) announced today a major development in the global crypto derivatives market, as an estimated $20.6 trillion in liquidity is moving across trading venues in 2026. The announcement highlights a significant change in market behavior, with more traders choosing platforms based on execution quality, efficiency, and performance rather than platform size alone. This shift could reshape competition across the industry and redefine how liquidity is distributed in the market.

[According to CoinGlass, total crypto trading volume reached approximately $20.6 trillion in Q1 2026](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=uHFFFru4mOfTVCg2PPB0XNbf27YEKWPjdoumt2gHBzGAziDV7l8UlsCH3UQ1x-HjUTUgazW5iPoRoWHxPCzGipRtvhxzsXTcDJkkA1LiWJnMQHb5DQeunDKNKUT9Ha4EnPcKj-Zm1VPnSydeKl2oZkBVDpcR_gh2G4gLY7IRoRq7RcXrwkZ3tTcqtn-EpzOMtMSMncnacJIu_mieLvSC56uHRv1NoeuJpLkz4TjW-3j7RbEA2WfjZA7ExLGLfeZT), with derivatives accounting for around $18.6 trillion — nearly 90% of total activity. This structural imbalance highlights a broader transformation: crypto markets are increasingly driven by trading activity rather than long-term holding, where execution speed, liquidity quality and responsiveness directly impact outcomes.

![Zoomex](https://ml.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/8290c537-d3de-4147-8401-8f7382dec6f8/hjxdhsgd.png)

**The Redistribution of Global Flow**

Zoomex observes that in a derivatives-dominated landscape, liquidity is no longer a static resource held by a few legacy exchanges. [Recent industry moves reinforce this: the NYSE’s parent company taking a stake in crypto trading](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=r9gWsOuEk5v2zX94GYCjOwck0rtNKm9vjYwWyND6G69njoMwiu39fg6UzTKNtRrCWGVY1NsTWqqnhDEi7qfeCM_AMiP8XS3C8zIODl5_H-J3EOPpfKZoM-ASI8oGfwqEHP2QCwVD9T7dgiEpNNnuNYqIoSZKho_WXEWgoEsQxUKgVQ9vScTehZ_HN3XbjmKgjubJMUzO3U2BN0Ocm6QYpNafDppcL6LCUYtQNcQzahylmOkzCUSxyZJ4a1QnbbcDumLrGCCVG6E165Lf4NEERC2Z2W7Li40eK7Nht4pDppwcx74BZh8vYpTTTjarCXwED6PMHyOO_Ou-iSwouA20jg==), and Deutsche Börse’s heavy commitment to tokenized derivatives, signal that the competition has shifted from "asset listing" to "flow capture."


**"As traders become more selective, platform choice is increasingly defined by execution efficiency, usability, and trust,"** states the Zoomex team. **"Zoomex addresses this through a unified account structure that removes friction between spot and derivatives trading, combined with deep liquidity across more than 590 perpetual contracts to support stable execution."**

**Trading Depth Defines Execution Quality**

At the execution level, liquidity is not only about availability — it is about usability.

[Research from CryptoRank](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=CqBP2Z7oC2Z1E8n12Lr_oi8yk341mnwdgSMG9HgTUHrvdpsJAJd1m0WrysEX36ZicqzKicCivC18sL1_VCOBX-Lsx1ZHs9MW6qwhq8zZI6beGxzDTPolgDBauHWFcBNBSxrNh_-VPvlwUHngBIcMjw==) indicates that Zoomex demonstrates strong order book depth across major assets:

- BTC spot depth exceeding 62.7 million USDT
- ETH depth around 29.8 million USDT
- SOL depth surpassing 20.5 million USDT


In addition, execution tests show that a 1 BTC market order on Zoomex results in approximately 0.03% slippage.


![Zoomex](https://ml.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/47875dc5-803b-4bf9-93b9-4d818ca8f4ae/nxbcnmc.png)

These metrics suggest that traders can access more stable execution environments across multiple assets, helping reduce implicit trading costs and maintain consistency in strategy performance.

**Sub-10ms Latency: Eliminating Operational Friction**

In 2026, many execution inefficiencies stem not from the market, but from technical debt. Zoomex’s infrastructure is engineered to maintain **sub-10ms latency**, a critical benchmark that reduces slippage in fast-moving markets.

This technical edge is supported by a **unified account structure**, allowing traders to pivot between spot and derivative strategies without the delay of internal transfers. By shortening the distance between trading intent and execution, Zoomex solves the "operational friction" that often plagues legacy platforms.

**Simplicity as a Strategic Edge**

As trading environments become more complex, usability has emerged as a key differentiator. A 2025 study by the [**Financial Conduct Authority (FCA)**](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=sCDNIxX6YIv19kE5ccjiubr0Bx8JIfuWmFJCZopFFhD2X1CXm71Xxy4_s6fz9JXyNBts4cjsUDKxsZ9uteoK_EWCn8jzPcf0WgFa13hfR5GqMxRAuz0gKB4uKBXev7tUWP5VhHuiUXj5zsdcT3_YCjvSR9hKh_Je3i-0W2MHB9_TsNSnKbf8bhrQdeCyv6Bz0Dzhe6TOBc3hZBmfxsCFcw==) found that "ease of use" is now a top-tier factor in platform selection.

Zoomex aligns with this shift by offering a **simplified interface that reduces operational complexity**. However, simplicity does not come at the expense of transparency. **The system is designed to keep order execution and account records clear and verifiable, ensuring consistency in how trades are processed.**

**Hard-Coded Fairness: The "Glass-Box" Standard**

In an industry where trust is the ultimate currency, transparency must be more than a marketing slogan—it must be a technical reality. Zoomex believes that a platform's integrity is defined by the clarity of its fund flows and the impartiality of its execution engine.

"To Zoomex, transparency is more than a report; it’s a verifiable fund flow," the team emphasizes.


"Through our real-time asset display system and rigorous anti-manipulation protocols, we ensure that every trade is executed in a 'glass-box' environment, where fairness is hard-coded into the engine." By eliminating information asymmetry and preventing internal wash trading, Zoomex ensures that every participant—from individual retail traders to large-scale institutions—operates on a truly level playing field.

**The Next Phase: From Scale to Efficiency**

The next round of exchange competition will hinge on more than just a long menu of products. As derivatives continue to dominate and institutional participation expands, the deciding factor is shifting toward the **predictability of the trading experience.**

Zoomex’s positioning is clear: In a faster and more fragmented market, the winners will be those who minimize friction. By providing a **direct and predictable trading experience that aligns with how modern traders evaluate platforms**, Zoomex is not just participating in the liquidity shift — it is leading it.

**About ZOOMEX**

Founded in 2021, [Zoomex](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=7cS9GH4pFlIyTE8tJ60GPYj8er-aPEZiUBGw3Pp70TR9j2tz0wJWvJ1ASS2tPtXPyhh9lMXBARYYV5EfuymMTA==) is a global cryptocurrency trading platform with over 3 million users across more than 35 countries and regions, offering 700+ trading pairs. Guided by its core values of **“Simple × User-Friendly × Fast,”** Zoomex is also committed to the principles of **fairness, integrity, and transparency**, delivering a high-performance, low-barrier, and trustworthy trading experience.

Powered by a high-performance matching engine and transparent asset and order displays, Zoomex ensures consistent trade execution and fully traceable results. This approach reduces information asymmetry and allows users to clearly understand their asset status and every trading outcome. While prioritizing speed and efficiency, the platform continues to optimize product structure and overall user experience with robust risk management in place.

**As an official partner of the Haas F1 Team**, Zoomex brings the same focus on speed, precision, and reliable rule execution from the racetrack to trading. In addition, **Zoomex has established a global exclusive brand ambassador partnership with world-class goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez.** His professionalism, discipline, and consistency further reinforce Zoomex’s commitment to fair trading and long-term user trust.

In terms of security and compliance, Zoomex holds regulatory licenses including **Canada MSB, U.S. MSB, U.S. NFA, and Australia AUSTRAC, and has successfully passed security audits conducted by blockchain security firm Hacken.** Operating within a compliant framework while offering flexible identity verification options and an open trading system, Zoomex is building a trading environment that is **simpler, more transparent, more secure, and more accessible** for users worldwide.

# Microsoft and OpenAI Kill Exclusive Deal to Set Off an Explosive Billion-Dollar Cloud Rivalry

[![Annika Masrani](https://blog.tipranks.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Annika-Profile-1-96x96.png)\\
Annika MasraniApr 27, 2026, 10:14 AM](https://www.tipranks.com/news/author/annika-masranitipranks-com)

A+

A-

Story Highlights

- Microsoft stops paying revenue to OpenAI and removes the exclusive tag to let both firms fight for market share independently.
- OpenAI is already moving toward rival providers like Amazon to find the massive computer power it needs for its next-gen models.

![Microsoft and OpenAI Kill Exclusive Deal to Set Off an Explosive Billion-Dollar Cloud Rivalry](https://blog.tipranks.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/shutterstock_2248361481-750x406.jpg)

Microsoft [MSFT +0.05% ▲](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/msft) announced a major shift in its business on Monday, April 27, 2026, as it ends its exclusive agreement with [OpenAI](https://www.tipranks.com/private-companies/openai). The tech giant will no longer share revenue with its primary AI partner, marking a new phase for the most famous pairing in the industry. This move allows both companies to work with other firms to keep up with the fast-moving tech world.

### [Claim 55% Off TipRanks](https://www.tipranks.com/checkout/v2?subscription=tipranks-premium-yearly&llf=rss&coupon=INTRO55&checkoutPage=checkoutv2)

[Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade AMZN](https://www.tradretfs.com/AMZO?utm_source=TipRanks)

## **Microsoft Ends Revenue Payments**

The decision to change the agreement comes as both firms look to grow in different directions. Microsoft stated in a blog post that, “The rapid pace of innovation requires us to continue to evolve our partnership to benefit our customers and both companies,” proving that the old rules no longer fit the current market.

Microsoft previously held the sole rights to use OpenAI’s tools in its cloud, but those days are over. Despite the change, Microsoft still holds a 27% ownership stake in the startup, keeping it tied to the future of the firm.

## **OpenAI Pursues New Cloud Rivals**

OpenAI is now moving to work with other providers to get the computing power it needs. Building and running smart software requires a huge amount of digital space, and the startup is already talking to Amazon [AMZN -1.09% ▼](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/amzn) to expand its reach.

This change allows OpenAI to reach a much larger group of users without being locked into a single provider.

The market reacted quickly to the news on Monday morning. Microsoft shares dropped about 3% in early trading as investors weighed the loss of the exclusive deal. At the same time, Amazon shares gained about 1% as the market bet that OpenAI will move a large portion of its business to Amazon’s servers.

## **Is Microsoft a Good Stock to Buy?**

According to TipRanks, [Microsoft stock](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/msft) has received a Strong Buy consensus rating, with 33 Buys and two Holds assigned in the last three months. The [average 12-month MSFT stock price target](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/msft/forecast) is $570.30, suggesting a potential upside of 35.7% from the current level.

# Amazon Will Report Q1 Earnings on April 29 — Here’s Who Owns AMZN Stock

[![Shalu Saraf](https://blog.tipranks.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/cropped-1620635855415-96x96.jpg)\\
Shalu SarafApr 27, 2026, 06:30 PM](https://www.tipranks.com/news/author/shalu-saraf)

A+

A-

Story Highlights

- Amazon will report its Q1 FY26 earnings on Wednesday, April 29, after market close.
- Cathie Wood recently boosted her stake in the company, adding 280,450 shares worth roughly $71.5 million on Friday.
- Let’s explore AMZN’s shareholder structure through TipRanks’ ownership tool.

![Amazon Will Report Q1 Earnings on April 29 — Here’s Who Owns AMZN Stock](https://blog.tipranks.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/shutterstock_2258803661-1-750x406.jpg)

E-commerce and cloud computing giant Amazon [AMZN -1.09% ▼](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/amzn) is scheduled to announce its first-quarter 2026 earnings on Wednesday, April 29. [The stock has gained more than 14% so far this year](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/amzn), largely driven by the continued expansion and AI integration within AWS. Wall Street expects Amazon [to report EPS (earnings per share) of $1.63](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/amzn/earnings), reflecting 2.5% year-over-year growth. Meanwhile, revenue is projected to rise about 14% to $177.27 billion.

### [Claim 55% Off TipRanks](https://www.tipranks.com/checkout/v2?subscription=tipranks-premium-yearly&llf=rss&coupon=INTRO55&checkoutPage=checkoutv2)

[New trading tool for AMZN bulls](https://www.tradretfs.com/AMZO?utm_source=TipRanks)

Market sentiment remains highly positive, with several [top Wall Street analysts](https://www.tipranks.com/experts/analysts) maintaining their Buy ratings ahead of the release. Institutional interest has also ramped up, as seen with [Cathie Wood](https://www.tipranks.com/experts/hedge-funds/cathie-wood)’s recent activity. Through ARK Invest, [Wood added 280,450 shares of Amazon on Friday](https://www.tipranks.com/news/xe-amzn-amd-rklb-cathie-wood-bets-big-on-nuclear-ipo-and-amazon-trims-stakes-in-amd-and-rklb-4-25-26), a trade valued at approximately $71.5 million.

With Amazon stock back in focus ahead of earnings, it’s worth taking a closer look at who owns AMZN stock. Now, according to [TipRanks’ Ownership Tool](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/amzn/ownership), public companies and individual investors own 54.25% of Amazon. They are followed by exchange-traded funds ( [ETFs](https://www.tipranks.com/etf)), mutual funds, insiders, and other institutional investors, at 18.81%, 18.13%, 8.25%, and 0.56%, respectively.

[![](https://blog.tipranks.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/image-2342-1024x241.png)](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/amzn/ownership)

## **Digging Deeper into Amazon’s Ownership Structure**

Looking closely at Amazon’s top shareholders, founder and executive chair Jeffrey Bezos owns the highest stake at 8.22%. Next up is Vanguard, which holds a 6.65% stake in the company.

Among the top ETF holders, the Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF [VTI +0.14% ▲](https://www.tipranks.com/etf/vti) owns a 2.86% stake in AMZN, while the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF [VOO +0.16% ▲](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/voo) owns 2.32%.

Moving to mutual funds, Vanguard Index Funds holds about 5.86% of Amazon. Meanwhile, Fidelity Concord Street Trust owns 1.58% of the company.

## **Is AMZN Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?**

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Amazon stock based on 42 Buys versus three Hold recommendations. The average [AMZN stock price target](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/amzn/forecast) of $287.33 indicates 8.84% upside potential.

A push by major banks to offshore high-value jobs in software engineering and data science may signal the beginning of a manufacturing-style exodus of white-collar technology work from Australia, the head of international business at the University of Sydney Business School warns.

Vikas Kumar said the Commonwealth Bank’s decision to build large teams of technical experts in India – and other banks hosting teams in locations including Vietnam and the Philippines – was part of a broader trend to offshore capabilities in areas beyond call centre work.

![](https://static.ffx.io/images/$zoom_0.582%2C$multiply_2%2C$ratio_1.5%2C$width_756%2C$x_70%2C$y_0/t_crop_custom/c_scale%2Cw_620%2Cq_88%2Cf_auto/32866bee1622d576b2e36009793668a60dbaf608681d3fcfab239e6b33d41d87)

The Finance Sector Union says relocation of these specific banking roles creates a vulnerability that extends beyond the individual worker. Tim Beor

“I see that as a one-way movement – a complete brain drain,” Kumar said.

The Finance Sector Union said CBA employed 6788 people at its Indian campus – around 15 per cent of total headcount – up 138 per cent between 2022 and 2025. According to CBA, it is hiring for more than 65 roles based in Bangalore, almost all of them in tech development and maintenance.

“Some of the skills we need to deliver for our customers are difficult to find at scale in Australia – this is a broader industry challenge,” a CBA spokesman said. “We’ll always have a large workforce in Australia.”

Advertisement

CBA has had a campus in Bengaluru for [more than 20 years,](https://www.afr.com/companies/financial-services/cbas-indian-office-not-outsourcing-20040908-jlqna) and other major banks also have significant operations in Asia.

ANZ is in the process of [cutting 3500 domestic roles](https://www.afr.com/companies/financial-services/anz-flags-1-1-billion-profit-hit-ahead-of-results-20251031-p5n6qa) as it streamlines local operations. As of September 2025, it employed more than 42,698 full-time equivalent staff across 29 markets, including 20,899 in Australia, 6758 in New Zealand and 15,000 across the rest of the world. The bank declined to comment further, but its website says it maintains a 9000-strong Bengaluru campus described as a “technology and operations centre”.

National Australia Bank in March announced plans to [cut 170 domestic jobs](https://www.afr.com/companies/financial-services/nab-to-cut-170-jobs-across-business-bank-add-roles-in-vietnam-india-20260326-p5ziuq) while simultaneously adding 237 new roles in its offshore hubs in India and Vietnam. More than 73 per cent of NAB’s workforce resides in Australia, 14 per cent in New Zealand and 11.5 per cent in Asia.

Westpac has also been reshuffling its workforce, revealing plans in May last year to [axe 1500 people](https://www.afr.com/companies/financial-services/westpac-readies-the-axe-for-its-biggest-redundancy-round-in-years-20250520-p5m0om).

And earlier this month Bendigo and Adelaide Bank announced plans to [axe around 10 per cent of its staff](https://www.afr.com/companies/financial-services/bendigo-bank-to-slash-jobs-after-agreeing-tech-outsourcing-deals-20260409-p5zmfj), opting to outsource some to Indian tech giant Infosys. Bendigo Bank chief executive Richard Fennell said access to global capability and expertise would accelerate the pace of change at the bank. “By leveraging global expertise and becoming simpler and more efficient, we can reinvest in new technologies and capabilities,” he said.

FSU assistant secretary Nicole McPherson said all Australians should be deeply concerned by the trend. “Our banks are offshoring highly skilled jobs overseas to boost profits,” she said.

Advertisement

“Many are even deliberately outsourcing work to obscure the relationship between job losses and artificial intelligence – by outsourcing the work first, they can claim no jobs have been lost to AI.”

McPherson argued that the relocation of these specific roles created a vulnerability that extended beyond the individual worker.

“These are highly skilled bank workers who manage complex commercial relationships and deal with sensitive financial information, including risk and fraud functions. When banks lose essential staff and skill sets offshore, it leaves Australia exposed and ever more reliant on outsourced tech companies, which puts our entire economy at risk,” she said.

But RMIT associate professor of finance Angel Zhong said the trend to offshore high-level expertise was less about cost savings than accessing the right talent as businesses tried to scale their AI efforts.

“This isn’t just cost arbitrage any more,” she said.

A CBA spokesman said: “We maintain comprehensive business continuity and operational resilience plans designed to ensure we can continue serving customers during a wide range of potential disruptions – including natural disasters, technology outages or other external events.“

Advertisement

Research by consultants at BCG supports that position. The firm’s Sydney-based managing partner Nicole Sibilio said it was a trend for companies looking to scale expertise fast.

“The critical distinction is that this does not equate to a loss of control,” she said. “Strategic direction, architectural authority, and accountability for core decisions continue to sit with the home organisation. For Australian businesses, those decision rights and governance responsibilities remain firmly anchored in Australia.”

The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority is generally agnostic to these arrangements, provided the banks make sure core operations are maintained and data is stored securely.

While APRA mandates that management with Australian responsibilities reside locally, this only applies to very senior decision-makers. It leaves a regulatory grey area where the architects of a bank’s technical logic and AI systems might reside permanently offshore while their legal supervisors remain in Australia.

But firms argue a lack of local expertise means they have no choice. A report for the Australian Computer Society by Deloitte estimates Australia will need an extra 52,000 tech workers each year to meet 2030 demand forecasts.

Local financial services AI advisory and engineering firm Adaca is critical of the quality of graduates from Australian universities and has located the vast majority of its 175 employees, who include 150-odd developers, in the Philippines.

Advertisement

Founder Lambros Photios estimated that recruiting and onboarding a foreign tech worker into Australia would take his organisation between 18 and 24 months. “Software moves much faster than that,” he says. “Eighteen to 24 months from now, my technology initiatives will be very different to the ones that I have now.”

Cherie Wright is an immigration law expert at cross-border workforce consultancy Vialto. She said the skills-in-demand visa for “specialist” jobs was supposed to be processed by the Department of Home Affairs within a week, and for the more general category of “core skills” within 30 days. Processing times are currently closer to seven months, she says.

“Processing time delays are probably the number-one complaint that we get from our clients across the board, regardless of industry,” she said.

# Meta to cut 10% staff amid AI push


China Daily \|
Updated: 2026-04-25 06:22


![](https://img2.chinadaily.com.cn/images/202604/25/69ebed40a310d68600fc4f7d.jpeg)
The logo of Meta is seen at Porte de Versailles exhibition center in Paris, France, June 11, 2025. \[Photo/Agencies\]


SAN FRANCISCO — Meta Platforms plans to cut about 10 percent of its workforce, according to media reports on Thursday citing an internal memo, as the social media giant continues to pursue efficiency while increasing investment in artificial intelligence infrastructure and talent.

Meta had a global workforce of 78,865 employees as of Dec 31, 2025, according to its annual report, meaning the latest move would affect roughly 8,000 jobs. A source told Agence France-Presse that the layoffs will take place next month, with thousands more positions left unfilled.

In its latest annual report and earnings materials, Meta said it remains focused on "operating efficiently" while investing heavily in AI and infrastructure. The company reported quarterly costs of $35.15 billion in January, up 40 percent year-on-year.

Meta said it expects 2026 capital expenditures to be in the range of $115 billion to $135 billion, driven by investments supporting its Meta Superintelligence Labs efforts and core business, including data centers and computing capacity.

The move comes as cofounder and chief executive Mark Zuckerberg makes a priority of delivering "superintelligence" in a costly AI race against rivals including Amazon, Google, Microsoft and OpenAI.

Analysts say Meta is betting that heavy AI investment will boost advertising efficiency and unlock new revenue streams, including smart glasses developed with EssilorLuxottica. Some also expect further job cuts as AI tools increasingly automate tasks previously handled by large teams.

Similar adjustments are underway at other tech giants. According to media reports, Microsoft's chief people officer Amy Coleman introduced a one-time voluntary retirement program for a small percentage of the company's long-serving US employees, a move that could cover thousands of US employees.

As of June 30, 2025, Microsoft employed approximately 228,000 people on a full-time basis, including 125,000 in the United States, according to its annual report.

The company has also been spending aggressively on AI infrastructure. During its fiscal 2026 second-quarter earnings call in January, Microsoft said capital expenditures reached $37.5 billion, with roughly two-thirds of that spending allocated to short-lived assets, primarily GPUs and CPUs.

The moves underscore how major US technology companies are continuing to adjust their workforce structures and cost bases while pouring more resources into AI-related infrastructure, products and technical talent.

XINHUA — Agencies

# Survey: AI to affect personnel moves, totals in half of companies

By KAE MORISHITA/ Senior Staff Writer

April 27, 2026 at 18:18 JST

- Share
- [Tweet](http://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/16528957&text=Survey%3A%20AI%20to%20affect%20personnel%20moves%2C%20totals%20in%20half%20of%20companies%20%7C%20The%20Asahi%20Shimbun%20Asia%20%26%20Japan%20Watch)[list](https://twitter.com/search?q=https://www.asahi.com/ajw/articles/16528957)

- Print

![Photo/Illutration](https://p.potaufeu.asahi.com/71a4-p/picture/30782295/639d73fffc656dc05ff31ddaf1c19f43.jpg)

About half of companies said the introduction of generative artificial intelligence will change how they use human resources, including transferring personnel and putting a freeze on hiring, a survey showed.

The trend is more prominent among larger companies.

Tokyo Shoko Research Ltd. conducted the survey on 6,327 companies this month, and 2,088 of them said they were aggressively moving toward introducing generative AI to the workplace.

Twenty-nine percent of the 2,088 companies said the greater efficiency created through generative AI raised the possibility that employees would be shifted to other departments over the next five years.

Sixteen percent of them said the overall number of employees could be limited over that period.

Among large companies with at least 100 million yen ($627,000) in capital, 47 percent said they would likely shift personnel, while 11 percent said they would restrain the total workforce.

But only about 4 percent of the companies mentioned the possibility of seeking early retirement among white collar workers.

“The effects of AI on employment will likely emerge in a greater way among white collar employees at large companies,” said Kosuke Honma, a researcher at Tokyo Shoko Research who conducted the survey.

Of the 6,327 companies surveyed, 34 percent said they were promoting use of generative AI in business operations either in the company as a whole or in certain departments.

Among large companies, the figure was 59 percent.

In a similar survey conducted in August 2025, 25 percent of all companies and 43 percent of large companies were pushing the use of AI.

The latest results show a major change regarding AI use in under a year.
